title: System Requirements for Unix and Linux
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on Unix and Linux Platforms
System Requirements for Unix and Linux
text: System Requirements for Unix and Linux Uniface supports many different platforms and databases, but not all products and functionality are available on each platform. For the supported platforms and databases, consult the Platform Availability Matrix . For hardware requirements, see the readme.txt file in the Uniface installation directory, or the readme_unix.htm file on Rocket Community: Downloads . The products you can use depend on your license. For information on configuring database and network connectors, and other integration support, see Uniface documentation, and consult your database and third-party product documentation. User Environment When installing Uniface on Unix platforms, you need to create a user ID and home directory, and ensure that setup scripts for DBMS, network, or GUI products are executed. If the Uniface Router and the Uniface Server are to run under different accounts, the Uniface Router ( urouter ) needs to run under the root account. Licenses Uniface license management is usually handled by the Sentinel Rights Management System (RMS) using its License Manager. For Linux it is possible to use Sentinel cloud-leased licenses. For more information, see Uniface License Management . During installation, you are prompted for the type of Sentinel license. If a license server or license file is chosen, the file or server name is written in usys.asn : $license_options LM_LICENSE_FILE=… . When installing Linux with Sentinel cloud-leased licenses, the installation program also prompts you for the Entitlement ID you received from Uniface. This information is added to insunis . Web Server The default installation includes a fully configured Apache Tomcat HTTP Server and servlet engine.To use it, you need to configure the username and password, and Uniface Router port number for the Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) in uniface/webapps/uniface/WEB-INF/web.xml . If you already have your own web server, you can configure it to run Uniface applications after installation. Uniface web-based applications can be used with any web server capable of connecting to a servlet engine that is compliant with the 6.0 Jakarta servlet specification. These include: Apache Tomcat HTTP Server and servlet engine IBM WebSphere IBM HTTP Server Related Links Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages. TCP Connector The TCP connector provides network connectivity via the TCP/IP protocol. It handles unsecured IPv4 and IPv6 connections. It also establishes the physical connection for secure communication using the TLS connector. Installing Uniface on Unix and Linux The Uniface installation program and related files are provided in a .tar file. After extracting the file you can run the Uniface installer.
